sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: (QTOC) Movement as an Input in Quant Signal Sets
feed.summary: This article provides an AI-generated analysis for Innovator Growth Accelerated Plus Etf (NASDAQ: QTOC), highlighting neutral near and mid-term readings but a positive long-term bias. It outlines distinct institutional trading strategies, including position, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis with support and resistance levels. The report emphasizes an exceptional 11.3:1 risk-reward short setup, signaling a potential 3.2% downside against a minimal 0.3% risk.

feed.title: Stephens raises RBB Bancorp stock price target to $29 on earnings beat
feed.summary: Stephens has increased its price target for RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ: RBB) to $29 from $26, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, following the bank's strong second-quarter earnings. RBB Bancorp reported operating EPS of $0.60, surpassing both Stephens' estimate and the Street's consensus, and the stock is considered undervalued with a low PEG ratio. The bank also announced a 1 million share buyback program and expects mid-to-high single-digit net loan growth for the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Jensen Huang Turns to Japan’s Robots for Nvidia’s Next Growth Engine
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is focusing on Japan's robotics and manufacturing sector as the next major growth area for the company, following its significant success in generative A.I. During a recent visit to Tokyo, Huang promoted "physical A.I.," emphasizing Japan's strong potential to combine its precision manufacturing with Nvidia's A.I. chips and software to lead this new industrial revolution. This initiative includes a partnership with a Japanese A.I. consortium to build a national physical A.I. infrastructure, aiming to leverage A.I. to address Japan's labor shortages and secure a dominant position in the global A.I.-robotics market.

feed.title: Roper Technologies raises annual profit forecast on AI software demand
feed.summary: Roper Technologies has increased its full-year adjusted profit forecast and revenue growth outlook due to strong demand for its AI-integrated software products. The company's CEO, Neil Hunn, highlighted the launch of new AI-enabled products, which expanded its addressable markets. Roper also reported second-quarter revenue and adjusted EPS that exceeded estimates, while also expecting a strong third quarter.

feed.title: Global Payments Schedules 8 a.m. Webcast for Q2 Results
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (NYSE: GPN) is scheduled to release its second-quarter financial results before the market opens on Wednesday, August 5, 2026. The company will also host a live audio webcast at 8:00 a.m. ET to discuss these results. Interested parties can access the webcast and its archived replay via the investor relations page on Global Payments' website.

feed.title: FDA clears Johnson & Johnson’s surgical robot for ‘the next era in surgery’
feed.summary: The FDA has approved Johnson & Johnson's Ottava Robotic Surgical System for several soft tissue surgeries, enabling J&J to compete with established players like Intuitive Surgical and Medtronic. J&J plans a limited commercial launch in the U.S. initially, focusing on customer success before expanding into more indications and regulatory jurisdictions. Despite Ottava's unique, space-saving design, analysts note that Intuitive Surgical's da Vinci robot remains the preferred choice due to its strong physician relationships and integration into hospital workflows.

feed.title: Tractor Supply misses estimates, cuts guidance on weak demand
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company reported second-quarter results that fell short of analyst expectations and subsequently lowered its full-year outlook. The rural lifestyle retailer cited unusually adverse conditions in May, especially softness in seasonal and discretionary categories, as the primary reason for missing its sales targets. Consequently, the company updated its fiscal 2026 guidance, projecting lower adjusted EPS and net sales growth.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoran earnings beat by $0.15, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoran (NYSE: FCX) announced second-quarter earnings per share of $0.74, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.15, and revenue of $7.03 billion, exceeding the consensus estimate of $6.71 billion. The company's stock has seen a 6.47% increase in the last three months and a 45.81% rise over the past year, indicating "good performance" according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern earnings beat by $0.29, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations with an

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) Beats Expectations in Strong Q2 CY2026, Stock Soars
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) reported strong Q2 CY2026 results, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS expectations. The company saw a 13.8% year-on-year revenue increase to $872.3 million and an adjusted EPS of $2.37, beating analyst estimates by 13.9%. Following the positive report, the company also raised its full-year revenue and adjusted EPS guidance, causing its stock to rise by 7.3%.

feed.title: Skyworks launches network synchronizers for AI data centers By Investing.com
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc. has introduced its new SKY6911x/2x NetSync network synchronizers designed for AI data centers and high-speed communications infrastructure. These devices integrate advanced synchronization features, including IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol, and support high-speed SerDes interfaces. The launch aims to reduce system complexity and accelerate development for modern network infrastructure requiring precise timing.

feed.title: KeyCorp Raises Block (NYSE:XYZ) Price Target to $105.00
feed.summary: KeyCorp raised its price target for Block (NYSE:XYZ) to $105.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating, which suggests a 35% upside. This adjustment follows Block's strong Q2 earnings report, where the company surpassed EPS and revenue estimates and provided positive FY2026 guidance. While analyst sentiment is generally positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus, insider selling activity has been noted over the last 90 days.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (NSC) Norfolk Southern Corporation Posts Q2 Adjusted EPS $3.52 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $3.31
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern Corporation (NSC) reported strong second-quarter results, with adjusted EPS of $3.52 per share, exceeding FactSet's estimate of $3.31. The company's revenue also rose, reaching $3.5 billion, driven by increased freight demand and fuel surcharges. This positive performance was in line with other railroad operators like Union Pacific, which also posted higher quarterly profits.

feed.title: Why is Huntington Bancshares stock sliding today? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) stock fell 3.4% in pre-open trading after its Q2 2026 earnings missed Wall Street expectations, particularly on earnings per share. This disappointment, coupled with a recent downgrade by Jefferies, negative insider selling activity, and a challenging economic environment, contributed to the selling pressure. Despite some analysts maintaining "buy" ratings, the weak Q2 results failed to alleviate concerns about the bank's margin trajectory and integration execution.

feed.title: Second Quarter Highlights
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin's second quarter of 2026 was marked by significant investments in innovation and manufacturing, expanding its defense industrial base with new facilities for THAAD and Next Generation Interceptors in Alabama. The company strengthened partnerships with GM Defense, secured contracts for F-16 Block 70 aircraft and Space-Based Interceptor capabilities, and invested in counter-UAS technologies, demonstrating rapid advancements across air, space, and missile defense sectors. These efforts underscore Lockheed Martin's commitment to national security, economic growth, and supporting military personnel.

feed.title: Stifel Adjusts Price Target on Teledyne Technologies to $775 From $750, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Stifel has increased its price target for Teledyne Technologies (TDY) from $750 to $775, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions, with BNP Paribas also raising its price target for Teledyne to $760. The company recently reported strong Q2 2026 earnings, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS estimates, and subsequently raised its full-year profit forecast.

feed.title: Citizens Financial (CFG) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) reported strong Q2 2026 results with a 15% sequential increase in EPS, driven by significant revenue growth, including a 4.4% rise in net interest income and 8% in fee income. The bank is progressing with its Private Bank expansion and "Reimagine the Bank" program, which aims for $450 million in annualized pre-tax benefits by 2028. Management provided positive guidance for Q3 and the full year, anticipating continued NIM expansion and operating leverage.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton funds report Q2 share repurchase activity By Investing.com
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton announced share repurchases for two closed-end funds during the second quarter of 2026. The Templeton Dragon Fund, Inc. repurchased 92,000 shares, bringing its total repurchases to over 9.6 million shares since inception, while the Templeton Emerging Markets Fund bought back 5,500 shares, totaling over 3.1 million shares. These repurchases occur when the funds are trading at a discount to their net asset value, benefiting common shareholders by increasing NAV accretion.

feed.title: UnitedHealth, Ardagh Metal Packaging, Nvidia And More: CNBC’s ‘Final Trades’
feed.summary: This article summarizes stock recommendations from CNBC's 'Final Trades', featuring UnitedHealth Group, Ardagh Metal Packaging, Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF, and NVIDIA Corporation. It highlights UnitedHealth's strong Q2 results and raised guidance, Ardagh Metal Packaging's high yield and analyst rating, and NVIDIA's expansion of its AI Agent Toolkit. The piece also includes the recent price action for each of the mentioned stocks.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (FCX) Freeport-McMoRan Posts Q2 Adjusted EPS $0.74 vs. FactSet Estimate of $0.62
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) has reported its Q2 adjusted EPS of $0.74, which surpasses the FactSet estimate of $0.62. This indicates a stronger performance than anticipated by analysts for the second quarter. The company specializes in copper, gold, and molybdenum mining, with operations globally.

feed.title: Cummins delays full production of new engines to late 2027 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. announced a delay in the full production of its Model Year 2027 X10 and X15 engines to late 2027, citing a review of proposed EPA emissions regulations. The company will phase in limited production starting January 2027 and maintain current models during the transition to ensure customer support and product confidence. Despite the delay, Cummins confirmed its plan to launch the next-generation B platform in January 2028.

feed.title: Penske Automotive Group (NYSE:PAG) Rating Lowered to "Neutral" at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup downgraded Penske Automotive Group (NYSE:PAG) from a "buy" to a "neutral" rating, setting a $210 price target. This comes amidst mixed analyst views, an unsolicited take-private proposal from Penske Corporation and Mitsui, and the company's 23rd consecutive quarterly dividend increase. Insider selling by CFO Michelle Hulgrave and Director Greg C. Smith was also noted.

feed.title: What Could Keep Apple Stock Climbing?
feed.summary: Despite a 54% run-up in a year, Apple's stock could continue climbing due to the historic success of the iPhone 17 family, which is driving significant revenue growth. While rising memory costs pose a potential headwind, the unprecedented demand for Apple's flagship product appears strong enough to absorb these pressures. The company's accelerating total revenue growth and optimized operating margins suggest a robust financial position capable of sustaining further upside.

feed.title: Chipotle opens first Mexico restaurant in Monterrey
feed.summary: Chipotle has opened its first restaurant in Mexico, located in San Pedro Garza García, Monterrey, in partnership with Alsea. This marks the brand's entry into Latin America's largest Spanish-speaking market, with plans for further expansion in Nuevo León and Mexico City. The move contributes to Chipotle's growing international presence across multiple continents.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies Inc (KEYS) Q4 2025 Earnings Report - Results, Call & Slides
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations with an actual EPS of $1.91 against a consensus of $1.83 and actual revenue of $1.42 billion, beating the expected $1.38 billion. The company recorded a year-over-year revenue growth of 10.26%. The next earnings report is estimated for August 19, 2026.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan beats quarterly profit estimates on higher copper prices
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan surpassed Wall Street's profit estimates for the second quarter due to a significant rise in copper prices, which helped offset reduced production at its Grasberg mine in Indonesia. Copper prices climbed 41.5% year-on-year, driven by supply concerns and resilient demand from China, while operations at the Grasberg mine are gradually recovering after a flood incident. The company reported an adjusted profit of 74 cents per share, exceeding analyst expectations of 59 cents.

feed.title: 12 Information Technology Stocks Moving In Thursday's Pre-Market Session
feed.summary: This article lists 12 information technology stocks that showed significant movement in Thursday's pre-market session. It details both gainers like Domo, NVE, and Richardson Electronics, with some reporting recent earnings, and losers such as STMicroelectronics and Abits Group. The report includes their respective stock price changes, current prices, and market capitalization.

feed.title: Pharma Pulse: Cardinal Health Bulks Up Home Care, Trump Unveils Phased Generics Tariffs
feed.summary: Cardinal Health expanded its home care business with two acquisitions totaling $360 million, focusing on diabetes health and urology to strengthen its direct-to-patient services. Separately, President Trump announced a phased tariff schedule for generic drugs, initiating with zero tariffs until August 2026, escalating to a 200% levy by 2029 to encourage domestic manufacturing. The article also covers Novo Nordisk's lawsuit against Eli Lilly, accusing Lilly of false advertising for its GLP-1 drugs Zepbound and Mounjaro by misrepresenting comparative efficacy against older, lower-potency Novo formulations.

feed.title: PLNT INVESTOR DEADLINE APPROACHING: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Planet Fitness Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit Deadline on September 14, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is reminding Planet Fitness investors of the upcoming September 14, 2026 deadline to seek lead plaintiff status in a federal securities class action lawsuit. This lawsuit alleges that Planet Fitness made false and misleading statements regarding its customer acquisition and marketing metrics, leading to a significant drop in stock price after the company announced disappointing Q1 2026 results. Investors who purchased PLNT common stock between November 8, 2023, and May 6, 2026, may be eligible to participate.

feed.title: Brown & Brown partners with Anthropic, McKinsey and Accenture
feed.summary: Brown & Brown is embarking on an AI-first enterprise transformation, partnering with Anthropic, McKinsey & Company, and Accenture. This initiative aims to integrate AI across its operations, empowering its 23,000 teammates and deploying Anthropic’s Claude for various functions, including customer service and software development. The company emphasizes that AI will enhance employee capabilities rather than replace them, focusing on responsible implementation and specialized solutions.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial declares $1.70 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial, Inc. (NYSE:AMP) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.70 per common share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on August 21, 2026, to shareholders of record as of August 3, 2026. This decision was approved by the company’s Board of Directors.

feed.title: Union Pacific beats Q2 estimates on volume growth
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) exceeded Q2 adjusted earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $3.41 EPS against an estimated $3.19, and $6.9 billion in revenue against $6.65 billion. The strong performance was driven by volume growth and core pricing gains, leading to a 12% year-over-year revenue increase and a 6% rise in net income. The company also raised its 2026 EPS growth outlook to high-single digits, affirming its commitment to operational improvements and strong cash generation.

feed.title: Freeport Results Webcast Includes Slides, Replay Through Aug. 21
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE: FCX) has released its second-quarter and six-month 2026 financial results, which are available on its Investor Relations website. The company is hosting a conference call and webcast with slides for securities analysts today at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss these results. A replay of the webcast will be accessible until Friday, August 21, 2026.

feed.title: Cummins Announces Phased Model Year 2027 Product Launch Plan
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. announced a phased launch plan for its Model Year 2027 X10 and X15 engines, adapting to proposed changes in U.S. EPA emissions regulations. This strategy allows for a measured production ramp-up, maintaining regulatory compliance and supporting customer production schedules while keying on quality. The current X15, X12, and L9 engines will remain available during the transition, with the next-generation B platform scheduled for a 2028 launch.

feed.title: United Rentals, Inc. (NYSE:URI) Announces $1.97 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: United Rentals, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $1.97 per share, payable on August 26th to stockholders of record on August 12th. This dividend represents an annualized yield of approximately 0.8% and is well-covered by the company's earnings, with a payout ratio of 16.6%. The company recently exceeded quarterly earnings and revenue estimates and maintains a "Moderate Buy" average analyst rating with a $1,140 price target.

feed.title: GE (GE) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: GE Aerospace reported strong Q2 2026 results, with adjusted revenue and earnings both growing over 20% year-over-year, leading to increased financial guidance for the full year. The company's performance was driven by robust commercial services demand and operational improvements from its FLIGHT DECK system, despite ongoing supply chain pressures. Key achievements include improved LEAP engine turnaround times and significant progress in next-generation engine technologies, underpinning confidence in continued growth.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton Closed-End Funds Announce Share Repurchases for the Second Quarter of 2026
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton's closed-end funds, specifically Templeton Dragon Fund, Inc. (TDF) and Templeton Emerging Markets Fund (EMF), announced their share repurchase activities for the second quarter of 2026. TDF repurchased 92,000 shares, and EMF repurchased 5,500 shares, with a total of 9,612,616 shares and 3,160,871 shares repurchased respectively since each fund's program inception. These repurchases aim to enhance shareholder value by buying back shares when trading at a discount to Net Asset Value (NAV), leading to incremental accretion to the NAV for common shareholders.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Corp. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.30 (NYSE:LVS)
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE:LVS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share, payable on August 12th to shareholders of record on August 4th, representing an annualized yield of about 2.7%. Despite mixed Q2 earnings—missing analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue—the company continues to return capital to shareholders through dividends and $787 million in stock repurchases, increasing its buyback authorization to $6.0 billion. Analysts have adjusted price targets downwards due to caution regarding near-term demand, particularly in Macau.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp (USB) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp reported strong Q2 2026 earnings with diluted EPS up 22% year-over-year and total net revenue growing 10.1% to $7.71 billion, driven by higher net interest income and significant fee growth. The acquisition of BTIG contributed $98 million in its first month, bolstering capital markets revenue and accelerating the company's shift towards a fee-dependent business model. Management upgraded its full-year 2026 revenue guidance to 7% to 9% growth, reflecting sustained loan demand and strategic integrations, while maintaining strong capital and credit quality.

feed.title: Skyworks Introduces New NetSync™ Network Synchronizers
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions, Inc. has introduced its new SKY6911x/2x family of NetSync™ network synchronizers, designed for AI data centers and other high-speed communications infrastructure. These devices integrate network synchronization features, ultra-low jitter clock generation, and AccuTime™ software to simplify time-sensitive network designs. The NetSync devices aim to reduce system complexity, accelerate product development, and support increasing data speeds in modern network infrastructures.

feed.title: CSX Corporation Stock: Growing Through New Management Strategies (NASDAQ:CSX)
feed.summary: CSX Corporation reported in-line Q2 results with significant revenue and EPS growth, driven by strong market demand and operational efficiency. Management updated guidance to high single-digit revenue growth and consistent margin expansion, with a focus on truck-to-rail conversion and intermodal growth. Despite robust fundamentals and strategic investments, the author rates CSX a "hold" due to its fully valued stock price and technical overbought conditions, suggesting a wait for better entry points.

feed.title: PodcastOne (Nasdaq: PODC) Acquires The Magnificent Others with Billy Corgan
feed.summary: PodcastOne (Nasdaq: PODC) has acquired the exclusive sales and distribution rights to "The Magnificent Others," an interview podcast hosted by Billy Corgan of The Smashing Pumpkins. The acquisition aims to expand PodcastOne's premium portfolio, create new advertising opportunities, and strengthen its position in creator-led podcasts. The podcast, launched in 2025, has already amassed over 2 million downloads and 76 episodes featuring influential guests from various fields.

feed.title: Farsight AI Tool Will Build Pitch Decks From Firm Templates
feed.summary: S&P Global Market Intelligence announced a strategic partnership and minority investment in Farsight to integrate an AI-powered client-ready material creation capability into its Capital IQ Pro platform. This new add-on, expected later in 2026, will utilize Farsight’s tools, Capital IQ Pro data, and a firm's own templates to generate tailored pitch decks, confidential information memoranda (CIMs), and valuation materials. The initiative supports Kensho Data & Platforms’ strategy for AI-native workflows, aiming to enhance efficiency for bankers and investors.

feed.title: Park Aerospace Chooses Tulsa International Airport for New Advanced Composite Materials Facility
feed.summary: Park Aerospace Corp. has selected Tulsa International Airport's North Development Area for a new advanced composite materials manufacturing facility, investing approximately $65 million. The facility, expected to open in 2028 and create over 100 jobs, underscores Tulsa's growing reputation as a hub for aerospace innovation and advanced manufacturing. This announcement, made at the Farnborough International Airshow, highlights the airport's collaborative ecosystem, talent pipeline, and strategic location.

feed.title: Here Are Thursday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: AT&T, Applied Digital, Autodesk, Duke Energy, Exxon Mobil, JPMorgan Chase & Company, Live Nation Entertainment, and More
feed.summary: This article details Thursday's top Wall Street analyst research calls, including upgrades, downgrades, and new initiations for several major companies like AT&T, JPMorgan Chase, and Exxon Mobil. It also provides a snapshot of pre-market stock futures, bond yields, commodity prices (oil, gold), and cryptocurrency trends, highlighting ongoing concerns about inflation and geopolitical instability impacting markets.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on Keurig Dr Pepper to $38 From $33, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has raised its price target for Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) to $38 from $33, while reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the non-alcoholic beverage producer. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments for Keurig Dr Pepper.

feed.title: Union Pacific: Q2 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corp. (UNP) reported strong second-quarter results, with net income reaching $1.99 billion, or $3.36 per share. Adjusted earnings of $3.41 per share surpassed Wall Street expectations of $3.20 per share. The railroad also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $6.86 billion against an expected $6.65 billion.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor Q1 earnings seen lower; analysts revise targets
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corporation is projected to report lower first-quarter earnings, with analysts estimating 87 cents per share on revenue of $1.02 billion. The company will release its Q1 FY27 earnings report after the closing bell on Thursday, July 23. Several analysts have adjusted their price targets and ratings, with some upgrading the stock while others maintained or downgraded their positions.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics raises full-year guidance after Q2 revenue rises 10.2%
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics reported strong Q2 2026 financial results, with net revenue increasing by 10.2% to $3.043 billion and adjusted diluted EPS rising 19.1% to $3.12, both surpassing analyst estimates. Attributing the growth to execution across various channels and sustained demand, the company raised its full-year 2026 guidance for revenue to $11.95 billion–12.05 billion and adjusted diluted EPS to $11.05–$11.25, following a favorable resolution of tax contingencies. Operational highlights included advancing Co-Lab Solutions, new kidney care capabilities, and double-digit growth in Advanced Diagnostics like the Quest AD-Detect blood test.

feed.title: INVESTOR DEADLINE APPROACHING: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Insulet (PODD) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit Deadline on August 31, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential claims against Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) and reminds investors that the deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action lawsuit is August 31, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Insulet made false or misleading statements regarding its manufacturing controls and procedures, leading to product recalls and significant stock price drops. Investors who purchased Insulet securities between February 21, 2023, and May 26, 2026, may be eligible to participate.
